quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

intent: Prevent layout breakage, misalignment, or jarring visual seams when generic modules stack against locked, brand-mandated templates.
rule_text: Sections adjacent to locked components (such as the header, footer, or ISI) must not include decorative elements that visually rely on continuity with the adjacent component (e.g., a wave effect intended to carry over into the footer).

quotes: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section**
self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding
Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.
Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; no margins outside the section — assembly is direct stacking.
Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**

intent: Maintain email client compatibility and consistent vertical rhythm in stacked modular templates.
rule_text: Each section is a self-contained 600px block (dimension.canvas.width = 600px) with its own top/bottom 32px padding (padding.section.vertical = 32px) and no margins outside the section to enable direct stack assembly.

quotes: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

intent: Ensure automated programmatic assemblers have the metadata keys needed to prevent collision and formatting errors.
rule_text: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes dermatomyositis (DM), footnote symbols consumed, and whether it contains a CTA.
